game_name: Star Wars Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II

danger_signals.visual_form: White armor-clad humanoid soldiers with distinctive silhouettes
danger_signals.meaning: Active Empire presence and immediate combat threat; established enemy faction

danger_signals.visual_form: Force-using enemies with lightsabers and dark aura effects
danger_signals.meaning: High-level threat; story antagonists; Force-sensitive danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental fauna and creatures native to hostile areas
danger_signals.meaning: Territorial danger; environmental hazard to player progression

danger_signals.visual_form: Mobile enemy assets with distinct silhouettes
Incoming projectile streams visible to player
danger_signals.meaning: Active combat state; lightsaber tool function becomes defensive necessity

safety_signals.visual_form: Friendly character models and avatars that do not attack
safety_signals.meaning: Safe interaction zones; moral choice indicator (light side path via non-harm)

safety_signals.visual_form: Lightsaber glow providing ambient light in dark areas
safety_signals.meaning: Safe navigation tool; dual-purpose utility and safety beacon
Recovery and healing area; story-safe zone

safety_signals.visual_form: Transition to next level after objective completion
safety_signals.meaning: Safe advancement; temporary reprieve from immediate threat

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Ally faction; player works as mercenary for this faction
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Medical frigates; command structures; recently established authority zones

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Imperial stormtroopers with distinctive white armor; hostile presence
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Empire-controlled territories; Jerec's reformation plan sites; occupied workshops

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Dark-side Force wielders with unique lightsaber colors and effects
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Valley of the Jedi location; Morgan Katarn's workshop (prior occupation); interrogation sites

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Treacherous allies; double-crossing NPCs with deceptive appearances
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Nar Shaddaa lower levels; mercenary meeting points

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Post-Return of the Jedi (5 ABY) - Empire weakened, New Republic ascendant
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mix of Imperial stormtrooper presence and New Republic authority structures; transitional power dynamics visible in environment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Empire attempting reformation through Dark Jedi agents; New Republic establishing command presence through frigates and mercenary partnerships

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Knowledge of Valley of the Jedi as contested prize
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dark Jedi pursuit through multiple planets; map locations and data disk significance; workshop occupation markers
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Active interrogation of Jedi Master Qu Rahn; systematic removal of map evidence; Force spirit guidance indicating spiritual importance

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Lightsaber acquisition post-level-three; Force power visual effects with light/dark/neutral color coding; avatar customization by player moral choice
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Escalating Force ability usage; branching story paths based on harm to NPCs; ending divergence (light vs. dark side manifestation)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dark area presence requiring lightsaber illumination; monster concentrations in environmental hazard zones; vehicle presence indicating heavy combat zones
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Environmental puzzle requirements; secret locations indicating hidden safe paths; objective-gated progression through threat areas

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Force power color selection (blue/green for light, red for dark, neutral variations); lightsaber color customization reflecting path
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPC reactions to player harm; ending cinematic variance; star allocation to conflicting power types triggering story branching

environmental_storytelling.example: Morgan Katarn's workshop where Dark Jedi removed the Valley map before Kyle's arrival, showing prior enemy activity and temporal narrative layering
Morgan's data disk containing Valley location information, recovered from Nar Shaddaa's lower levels, serving as macguffin and plot driver
Encounter with family droid WeeGee in workshop, establishing personal history and emotional stakes through familiar non-hostile entity
Nar Shaddaa lower levels vs. medical frigate vs. Sulon vs. Valley sites indicate power structure and danger progression
8t88 information broker presenting as ally before shuttle escape attempt, marking Nar Shaddaa as deceptive/treacherous zone
Qu Rahn's Force ghost visitation during Kyle's recovery establishes Jedi pathway and moral/spiritual dimension of world state

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Hostile engagement with player; coordinated attack formations; blaster fire requiring lightsaber deflection
Systematic elimination of Jedi resistance; map recovery operations; Force power usage indicating superior threat level
No attack response; dialogue interaction possible; moral choice indicator (harming = dark path indicator)
Initial information trade appearance followed by betrayal; escape attempt triggering pursuit sequence; severed arm detail indicating violence escalation
Disk decoding assistance; non-threatening presence; emotional connection marker
Medical frigate delivery; partnership management; implies safe harbor mechanics
Force ghost guidance during sleep; wisdom provision; path indication toward Jedi identity
Area-specific danger; non-faction-aligned; environmental navigation obstacles

notes: Visual communication heavily relies on Star Wars franchise established visual language: white Imperial armor = immediate enemy threat, lightsaber colors = Force alignment indicators, NPC harm = moral state tracking. World state communicates through environmental occupation (Dark Jedi in workshops), character positioning (mercenary vs. Jedi journey), and mechanical divergence (light/dark Force powers with gameplay consequences). Branching endings visually manifest player moral choices made throughout (NPC interaction harm, Force power allocation patterns). Environmental storytelling uses droid companions, artifact locations, and location safety variance to communicate narrative progression and danger zones.
